name: Petroleum Engineering
description: Petroleum Engineering focuses on the exploration, extraction, and production of oil and natural gas resources. It integrates geology, drilling technology, reservoir engineering, and production optimization for energy resource development.

subjects.name: Petroleum Geology
subjects.description: Study of sedimentary basins, source rocks, reservoir rocks, and trapping mechanisms for hydrocarbon accumulation. Covers geological mapping, seismic interpretation, and basin analysis for petroleum exploration.
learning_outcomes: Identify sedimentary rock types and depositional environments
Interpret seismic sections for structural and stratigraphic traps
Evaluate source rock potential using geochemical data
Construct geological cross-sections for basin analysis
subjects.industry_relevance: Foundation for exploration activities in oil and gas companies worldwide.

subjects.name: Reservoir Engineering
subjects.description: Covers fluid flow in porous media, reservoir characterization, and reserve estimation. Includes material balance methods, pressure transient analysis, and decline curve analysis for production forecasting.
learning_outcomes: Apply Darcy's law for single and multiphase flow in reservoirs
Estimate hydrocarbon reserves using volumetric and material balance methods
Analyze pressure transient data for reservoir properties
Forecast production using decline curve analysis
subjects.industry_relevance: Core discipline for field development planning and reservoir management in E&P companies.

subjects.name: Drilling Engineering
subjects.description: Study of drilling operations including drill bit design, drilling fluids, well control, and casing design. Covers directional drilling, cementing operations, and drilling hydraulics for safe and efficient well construction.
learning_outcomes: Design casing strings and cementing programs for well integrity
Calculate drilling hydraulics and optimize bit selection
Apply well control procedures to prevent blowouts
Plan directional and horizontal well trajectories
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for drilling contractors and oil and gas operating companies worldwide.

subjects.name: Production Engineering
subjects.description: Covers well completion, artificial lift systems, surface facilities, and production optimization. Includes multiphase flow in pipes, well stimulation techniques, and production system analysis.
learning_outcomes: Design well completions for different reservoir types
Select and design artificial lift systems for optimized production
Analyze nodal systems for production bottleneck identification
Design hydraulic fracturing and matrix acidizing treatments
subjects.industry_relevance: Directly responsible for maximizing hydrocarbon recovery in production operations.

subjects.name: Well Logging
subjects.description: Study of wireline and LWD logging techniques including resistivity, porosity, and gamma ray measurements. Emphasizes log interpretation for lithology identification, fluid saturation, and formation evaluation.
learning_outcomes: Interpret gamma ray, resistivity, and neutron-density logs
Calculate water saturation using Archie's equation
Identify lithology and pay zones from log responses
Correlate logs across multiple wells for field mapping
subjects.industry_relevance: Standard practice for formation evaluation in all oil and gas exploration and production wells.

subjects.name: Petroleum Refining
subjects.description: Covers crude oil distillation, conversion processes including catalytic cracking and reforming, and product blending. Includes refinery optimization, sulfur removal, and petrochemical feedstocks production.
learning_outcomes: Design atmospheric and vacuum distillation units
Analyze catalytic cracking and reforming process yields
Optimize refinery configuration for product slate targets
Evaluate environmental compliance for sulfur and emissions
subjects.industry_relevance: Core discipline for refinery operations, petrochemical complexes, and integrated energy companies.

subjects.name: Natural Gas Engineering
subjects.description: Study of natural gas properties, gas reservoir engineering, gas gathering systems, and processing plants. Covers gas dehydration, sweetening, liquefaction, and pipeline transportation of natural gas.
learning_outcomes: Calculate gas properties using equations of state
Design gas dehydration and sweetening facilities
Evaluate LNG liquefaction cycles and plant economics
Analyze gas reservoir performance and deliverability
subjects.industry_relevance: Growing demand for natural gas as cleaner fuel drives need for gas engineering expertise worldwide.

subjects.name: Enhanced Oil Recovery
subjects.description: Covers tertiary recovery methods including thermal, miscible, chemical, and microbial EOR techniques. Focuses on displacement mechanisms, sweep efficiency, and field-scale EOR project design.
learning_outcomes: Design thermal EOR projects using steam injection and in-situ combustion
Evaluate miscible flooding with CO2 and hydrocarbon solvents
Apply chemical EOR methods including polymer and surfactant flooding
Assess EOR project economics and recovery factor improvements
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for maximizing recovery from mature fields, with growing importance as easy oil reserves decline.

subjects.name: Offshore Drilling
subjects.description: Study of offshore drilling platforms, subsea systems, and marine operations for deepwater hydrocarbon extraction. Covers floating vessels, riser systems, station keeping, and offshore well completion techniques.
learning_outcomes: Compare fixed, compliant, and floating platform designs
Design subsea wellheads and production systems
Analyze riser behavior under environmental loading
Plan deepwater drilling campaigns with proper well control
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for deepwater exploration and production in basins like Gulf of Mexico, Brazil, and West Africa.

subjects.name: Pipeline Engineering
subjects.description: Covers pipeline design, construction, and operation for oil and gas transportation. Includes hydraulic analysis, material selection, corrosion protection, pigging operations, and integrity management.
learning_outcomes: Design pipelines for single and multiphase flow
Select pipeline materials based on fluid properties and operating conditions
Apply cathodic protection and coating systems for corrosion control
Develop pipeline integrity management programs
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical infrastructure discipline for oil and gas transportation networks spanning thousands of kilometers.

subjects.name: Well Testing
subjects.description: Study of pressure transient testing, drill stem testing, and production logging techniques. Covers test design, data acquisition, and interpretation using analytical and numerical well test models.
learning_outcomes: Design pressure buildup and drawdown tests for reservoir characterization
Interpret pressure derivative plots for reservoir model identification
Compute skin factor, permeability, and reservoir pressure from tests
Apply well test results to optimize field development plans
subjects.industry_relevance: Standard practice for reservoir characterization and well performance evaluation in the oil and gas industry.

subjects.name: Formation Evaluation
subjects.description: Integrated study of petrophysics, well logging, core analysis, and formation testing for reservoir characterization. Covers porosity, permeability, saturation, and net pay determination using multiple data sources.
learning_outcomes: Integrate log, core, and test data for reservoir characterization
Calculate petrophysical properties using multimineral analysis
Determine net pay and cutoffs for reserve estimation
Build petrophysical models for 3D reservoir modeling
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for accurate reserve booking and field development decisions in oil and gas companies.

subjects.name: Oil & Gas Economics
subjects.description: Covers economic evaluation of oil and gas projects including NPV, IRR, risk analysis, and production sharing contracts. Includes global energy markets, fiscal regimes, and portfolio management in E&P.
learning_outcomes: Evaluate upstream project economics using discounted cash flow methods
Analyze production sharing contracts and concession agreements
Apply Monte Carlo simulation for risk-based economic assessment
Assess global oil and gas market dynamics and price forecasting
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for investment decisions, project sanctioning, and business development in the energy sector.

subjects.name: Health Safety & Environment
subjects.description: Study of HSE management systems, risk assessment, and regulatory compliance in oil and gas operations. Covers process safety, environmental impact assessment, emergency response, and industry safety standards.
learning_outcomes: Apply HAZOP and risk assessment methodologies for process safety
Design emergency response plans for oil spill and blowout scenarios
Evaluate environmental impacts of drilling and production operations
Comply with international HSE standards including OHSAS and ISO
subjects.industry_relevance: Mandatory compliance area with increasing regulatory scrutiny across the global oil and gas industry.

subjects.name: Reservoir Simulation
subjects.description: Covers numerical modeling of fluid flow in reservoirs using finite difference and finite volume methods. Includes history matching, grid design, and prediction of reservoir performance under various development scenarios.
learning_outcomes: Build reservoir simulation models using industry-standard software
Perform history matching to validate simulation models
Predict field performance under different development scenarios
Optimize well placement and production strategies using simulation
subjects.industry_relevance: Standard tool for field development planning and reservoir management in major oil and gas companies.

subjects.name: Petrochemical Engineering
subjects.description: Study of petrochemical processes including steam cracking, catalytic reforming, and polymerization for producing olefins, aromatics, and polymers. Covers process design, catalysts, and downstream product applications.
learning_outcomes: Design steam cracking furnaces for ethylene production
Analyze polymerization processes for plastics manufacturing
Optimize catalyst selection for petrochemical reactions
Evaluate petrochemical project economics and market demand
subjects.industry_relevance: Drives the multi-trillion dollar plastics, fertilizers, and specialty chemicals industries worldwide.
